name: Christopher Ahuja for Congress
subject: The Momentum is Real in CA-32
body:
This moment is here--let's take it!

| | | | | [|
---](https://chrisahuja.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=67424df89870d1cac00e55f46&id=720b1304d2&e=53c7d12fc7)
| | | | | | Dear Peter,
**Our momentum in CA-32 is growing** — and voters across the district are ready for leadership that listens, shows up, and fights for working people.
Over the past few months, we’ve earned new community endorsements, built local grassroots support, and connected with voters who believe CA-32 deserves real solutions and a stronger voice in Congress.
Now, we’re taking a major next step.
[**We’ve officially filmed our first campaign commercia l**](https://chrisahuja.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=67424df89870d1cac00e55f46&id=ca1f209c9f&e=53c7d12fc7)—an exciting milestone and an opportunity to introduce a bold new vision for CA-32.
Filming the ad was only the beginning. Now we need to make sure voters actually see it.
[**Getting our message on television, online, and across the district takes real resources, and every contribution helps us reach more voters as we build this movement.**](https://chrisahuja.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=67424df89870d1cac00e55f46&id=de96cfede8&e=53c7d12fc7)
Because the truth is: people are struggling.
Families are being crushed by the cost of living. Rent keeps rising. Healthcare is unaffordable. Young people are drowning in student debt. Working families are being asked to do more with less while Washington continues to protect the status quo.
CA-32 deserves a fighter in Congress who will speak up about the issues impacting everyday people.
I’ll fight for:
• Medicare for all• Housing for all• Higher wages and union power• Tuition-free college• Cancel student debt• Green New Deal• Abolish ICE• Reproductive freedom• LGBTQ+ rights• End corporate corruption• Lock up the Epstein criminals
**This campaign is powered by people — not corporate PAC money.** If you believe it’s time for leadership that puts CA-32 first, I’m asking you to stand with us today.
[**Chip in $26 or whatever feels right — and help us get this message in front of voters across the district.**](https://chrisahuja.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=67424df89870d1cac00e55f46&id=7f13c823a5&e=53c7d12fc7) Thank you for believing in this movement and for helping us build something powerful together.**No PAC money. No AIPAC. Just people-powered change.**
---

I didn’t run for Senate to sit quietly on the sidelines. I’m a mom, a conservative, and a proud Alabamian—and I came to fight for the values that make America strong.

_**Faith. Family. Freedom.**_

The Left is doing everything they can to tear it all down. But I didn’t come to Washington to play games. I came to fight for the people who feel forgotten, overlooked, and ignored.

From securing the border to protecting our kids and defending our way of life—I’m in this for the long haul.

**This fight isn’t easy, but it’s worth it. And I’m not going anywhere.**

Will you stand with me?

clean_body: Seriously... Hello, We’re emailing to ask you to give $1 to help Democrat and retired Navy Rear Admiral Eileen Laubacher defeat Lauren Boebert. And no, that’s not a typo. Our next fundraising deadline is at midnight tonight, but we’re still very short of our $75,000 goal and what we need to send Boebert packing this November. But when your $1 is combined with thousands of other grassroots donors also chipping in to defeat Boebert, $1 can have enormous impact. $1 lets Eileen fill the tank in her car as she drives across the district, meeting with voters who are fed up with Lauren Boebert. $1 lets us invest in staff and critical resources needed to build a field and organizing campaign strong enough to win. $1 lets us fight back against the Super PACs and hold Boebert accountable. And $1 lets us take the lead and flip this seat that could decide whether or not Democrats take back control of the House for the next 2 years. name: Matsui Finance Team
subject: The GOP’s national budget cuts threaten our schools
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Matsui for Congress
body:
Public education has always been one of the clearest reflections of who we are as a country. | | | [](http://links.matsuiforcongress.com/s/c/-3T2NAZqRvDYdggFfToy77j-pExe_leyKw6JwFwXI_F8GV9X3UOeyf5DMuX4nvpo5xCfcU-7lR92RqD2zVhwboQtInIm_5HnfkcDdVWFknaBorwMu8VexcHVBDow6icGwo9WI_Drg6-_LEcHZvlP07IVZJTsoxPtRk1LQL3PpAVbGg51nVyxY-VJk0vfoRtfol0f6678wUw7vreirlMVvgNiI16DXp46d2mSCJotzJsbcP3uSml0uJbS9XJq4u8Lz8GTTPAuCZ6Z62Y_rsazwsVee5ypXLCX0DTUlPYXU9Wn83hfv_y0aXxGkVB7wiC0WYUb95m7NdR6_nJEh23Ny9OEKbwu9kAHUAIzI1riOy9KcCArr8nN9xwOdsRGGNIIdctHFYPVt8zYL2uxo8O44TSq4mKTO1tw43Q146rspzYvdRV6MUyOzSu_g033tN9R-5zerNJbYO14YnReuP9B8vCjv68QUdjUD0vKkF9t_ceCZvNs3J2PNzeITdwp3UhdPpn5dPz_73ZkfiSIju_JN0AVJwm9F5zW-7zELlI81aQUjR5I2eZAYkaJ2pG1z39SCGTG__XRaqW616LsnoePm5HybuVOP-qT0Vn9vQGfy_fbe5zXm1YFPWLsA2lvN0D1kkvaIReXTVt-W36Oqr_tqEVAeR_0zSXie5u0kHJtL1-Xvn04HKotEihbIvjfqAkTZuIS_hS0b0vSI8Hfhnr_fCp6atBknfqeu-Qsv2IHYd6nsb2PBYjAQtRfs9yibe1b5CPCwY26t4RKkH3Q3YOGSNEI5bAfUF1jWFz4RxoQFLn3BFqkpVNP6D_L6yTlw1ezI0e1qpw1_p8PW4l3L8GbureoEF9BIxg35POMmFThCVbXwvT5m9qSN6sGLSx7E5RldAH9uD0tbDf1tZPW8g/hF5trgBBrSPiv0xD16ZuI22jLIBXCw1-/20) Friend, Public education has always been one of the clearest reflections of who we are as a country. And it’s worth fighting for. In Congress, Doris has remained focused on strengthening our public schools—supporting teachers, investing in classrooms, and ensuring that every student has access to the tools they need to succeed in a modern economy. But Trump’s GOP has slashed and frozen federal funding for public schools. [Last July](http://links.matsuiforcongress.com/s/c/77kVNDB9BGMe_cIlWMDfSroffOe5UPHzHyXYLhj0FN4ZUSOCwp8Zj6cXQAB57JmnNHDMK6o-_lLSHcX3hhY_ne1JWVyTRdwRv-X-KLNp0xenLhUFRJQO9jXMO_O7BPTQ-i16xis3jcTCLyZGoK1jSJnz_aVk403iMonXIiGlWz0w-kAXMAe2hovR3W0IsdsF2V84PePK0qeYOPiKXgJcdyvTr4xNaISMX-yQg8--8AAj5wy5YO8SmX1w81XXaX6wTT3saqBU9OpKu85TA6c4uwkt9FRd3SWNFEZYsP4__4BeEtjiQxwr1CM5TWAKaeudTPYWl_-3jjy9ik8VseKyWLglKqDdwCUVvIfZ7nEKjS7PxxtL83TLnhQa-S7x_9aCEzEsNFfucGD1fZfAJGgXbMinDXRWX45YFojfjO9NwAUoFShjItYwH40KezWLT8V87Ka1YKSddD1FN0tysLj6k3j-s4D9pNsbUhWYD8wpfHcjv_8IOEPcE476z86aEY1i9ZbfgfPL3g/29evL9s-_F7kHJZCYCxJtaZYQfACM7Xv/20), his Department of Education withheld $833 million from the California Department of Education, and $232 million of that was appropriated directly to effective instruction. Right now, our schools are still feeling the effects of national education budget cuts. You may already know that the Sacramento City Unified School District is facing a [massive budget deficit](http://links.matsuiforcongress.com/s/c/zYm26L8TFJvupXkZae2de9o2qXfv-ArUGBMDF8E9pufFSZ2qcC81Ma_sZ7_LoUOCzUKHQI6UehXgND_lrQBRorE0_rYEIwn1h94Z6okcyzAgFL_3uxjKn4fQhvZzE3DVteVJw5v1QVF8hn3nv2bx3snhSRyZnXEkw9u7EYzjGmDQnoBS0QCyNtYTrqzojKKHuTsyDrRuwRUjea_zB8qU4m2UMP2p_juJ47Zf4rCNdwK58ppotSRqO4Sn4n9EF2Rg1P_xS8itRD0BAQrYYOCu3XrzI87RsT3Mywi5UZ7tWhlVYu5YECJ2UAjSZ8z8W5dVWQru-kM8py__hbnb1_PxgaNYrRjt7F2pnm_lNBRdJjK-WNQxKma247qU8Hz5ZVtyKRkfsR-tSFP4KMRFzrFHNZiX-NjKONUy668T-jU7jA9nEwLDZ52wSHtN25dCfesJ6jfLIep9JSOP1Dz-CkZGZ3Ql2_o0oElLJOUflM5luV60JE-quWhlhYQnFXMI/hUCyhJtXxKS6yTglLT77o35YmK4OU_Gl/20) and underenrollment, which are forcing them to **close up to four schools over the next five years.** [](http://links.matsuiforcongress.com/s/c/J1hZ3d2eznQwkNpVQW9mQw7IWruR7kz631CByeuyY8HBg1zNZ8BsYODHEGY64ZFpaQlvYtEC97uYhlHZf0d7orcQHeM_zm27tKlfGdvz0wqf44V4CEB7XCVRahNuMv2P0-aaYKwpeqxmNhyllwG68EHxiUI0tkx-5881vUnUtF0bz1LTgKWDdiWNRtWdCoJsvpo67BuPCb4gHlSCMm0-RL4OeqG9sf5nle7AUKdCYc69QCpkfm7ucbVzNMexjUY73OtVMaFOpc9H7hAwPmg0gUAjcl6X4M5R_YGxuOCT8liyM2GvJcQ3okOV-NeZuR0BhurzN-v52HASuJfa3eydvK_p7ZcBZvUB4IJ6aaa1lkt4cJetqxL3mnI5XzUikHp6obYDh3igAc1fKqlCCrMKndZgM8C6uCY4Ha4W-9IG_1-ZRm-eyiBSo50IL5Vz6RRmsqJfXWkrK80Jv7Loi1_Nx8SrDN_ZiABlAVgXhhLt_txnNu-ACVt-JucqTDMbAwExsIqwcUooko90u29OQ8F3FNNpZT9IX3ImW7EW8_28-DeghCa_FmTKgYXG4iVRH0vjwRrczXdduW3L83Qh36on3tHA9QPLs-1OYjrCw5JuY11QIvdVnVUUOI6XuIcbwRGe8zLpNVnMVSc7Qs9TTqYVsq1i-fajuZ2vbKbLbNmRu7D4L7fumzF115GH4IZhCgCJiLwucD8ktJkYcdAz5Hs58tK3xeeCR1key_1ciVdGVXNik01ZpgQxDAIC6WPKnen5CfhIPv1xqYPZQ5BxaVgolCM-t1i4-9nS5TnWp59OrkJN9LBmTfvdUJ9o-eGlbjq0Nfpw2Sxbt2uad4DXLHMs9P6Xp0Xm1jbZHQo18QqAazLYxyAxrx7QfuuPdoebqNW3H7KflR7eoNAR8rRofA/b5OUJJpnJ8MuSaGh1Qh_DWn9HYsFzwtZ/20) **This is unacceptable,** and it’s why we need to re-elect Representative Matsui. clean_body: Public education has always been one of the clearest reflections of who we are as a country. | | | Friend, Public education has always been one of the clearest reflections of who we are as a country. And it’s worth fighting for. In Congress, Doris has remained focused on strengthening our public schools—supporting teachers, investing in classrooms, and ensuring that every student has access to the tools they need to succeed in a modern economy. But Trump’s GOP has slashed and frozen federal funding for public schools. , his Department of Education withheld $833 million from the California Department of Education, and $232 million of that was appropriated directly to effective instruction. Right now, our schools are still feeling the effects of national education budget cuts. You may already know that the Sacramento City Unified School District is facing a and underenrollment, which are forcing them to close up to four schools over the next five years. This is unacceptable, and it’s why we need to re-elect Representative Matsui. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | ---|--- | | Thank you for standing with public schools. — Matsui Finance Team As a Member of Congress, Doris works to ensure that the voices of her district’s constituents are heard in Congress, and fights for the needs of the Sacramento region. Paid for by Matsui for Congress

name: Justin J. Pearson
subject: A political lynching
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Committee to Elect Justin J
body:
This is the latest news.
| [ ](https://jj-p.org/l/DaHIm0pfpDcw?t=NG0e0vdcNrFS)
---
Peter, I'm emailing you more motivated than ever before.

**Republicans passed their racist, outrageous gerrymandered districts in Tennessee that eliminate District 9 as the only majority-Black and majority-Democratic district in our state.**

They have cheated their way into a map that favors Republicans, where politicians are picking their voters instead of voters picking their leaders.

We need to call this what it is: **this new map is a political lynching** — an affront to justice and the fight for equality.

My heart aches for my hometown of Memphis, where I was born and raised and where generations of my family are buried. [](https://jj-p.org/l/VpjqT7XLNUPc) **But I refuse to give in to despair. Don't give up hope, Peter.** The fight still carries on, renewed with urgency as we work to restore the rights that our ancestors marched, prayed, bled, and died for.

[This righteous fight for our rights has never been more important, to push back against Jim Crow 2.0, to end the corruption that runs rampant and free, to give Black and Brown People a true voice in their democracy. Our fight continues: Will you please join me against authoritarianism and chip in $5 right away?](https://jj-p.org/l/5RSRGc70wA5a) | [CONTRIBUTE $5 »](https://jj-p.org/l/IEmBMKksLq1w)

The Supreme Court's ruling in _Louisiana v. Callais_ cleared the path for this, and I'm sure we're going to see the same playbook in other states soon, robbing Black and Brown People of their voting rights.

Republicans have taken the only Black-majority district from us. But I want you to know: No matter what they do, no matter how much they try to break us and make us bend and quit — **we will still be here.**

I'm still going to fight to demonstrate strong leadership in TN, and I hope that you, as a member of this People-powered movement, are still by my side.

This isn't over, and we will not stop until justice rolls down like waters and righteousness like an ever-flowing stream. [Will you chip in $5 or more in the next 30 minutes to fight this authoritarian power grab?](https://jj-p.org/l/Vbgi0RDrdBLc)

clean_body: This is the latest news. | Peter, I'm emailing you more motivated than ever before. Republicans passed their racist, outrageous gerrymandered districts in Tennessee that eliminate District 9 as the only majority-Black and majority-Democratic district in our state. They have cheated their way into a map that favors Republicans, where politicians are picking their voters instead of voters picking their leaders. We need to call this what it is: this new map is a political lynching — an affront to justice and the fight for equality. My heart aches for my hometown of Memphis, where I was born and raised and where generations of my family are buried. But I refuse to give in to despair. Don't give up hope, Peter. body:
Here's the truth, Peter: Karen Bass is scared, and she’s doing everything in her power to keep me out of City Hall. It’s almost as if she doesn’t want to see the city get any better. Crazy.

**At midnight, our fundraising numbers will be locked in, and I need you before that happens.**

Money talks in LA politics. It always has. A strong number tomorrow morning tells every big donor, every political insider, and every Bass advisor that Spencer Pratt is not going away.

I've watched this city collapse in slow motion. The homeless crisis got worse. The fires came. The excuses kept flowing.

**Bass has had years to fix it and hasn't.**

Angelenos don't need another career politician running out the clock.

**They need a fighter. I'm that fighter. But I need the resources to win.**

| [ HELP ME WIN ](https://links.trueamericanconservatives.com/s/c/OgcXHuuKFfTq1RfOYiyZVdF3mIsoo4YcDxUMGr974qfom1jBYYQigOzbwnQMYicgXPKqQUtreedeTCX-V_BW3nFrTuu9G4J2mjVPtQ018C5LyRHB8sKpD5wXVEKgHSKkmNx5M6VGuBghzdNruqwMNsIW9u0pvHj41gw6s0q1MDIkNNycJCFkmJLhc-vgEvCUSDk-Wu4B-V3ATyJIGHKcTBOXXALj9zXnD4ZjWRa0GzMMfuHnNbb6dhzTKkJht5ZkKjq5aYmg2PpKIiWHiuSxFusikvuqJv-gm_23NDfvYklB9EIakWM6gAvDt8869RoZZfjK9mx6oG9PCeB5bfxgzRPgrAfTcxDu1qvokIVnK8R2sz-ODxRuWyfK1TjMS-e2Ia9dSWhlGLt4Sbq8tOK5eYcKYPReqwxT-gb1gSOjLr37hHQ0CQmNHV92CHpt4-3pEwm4IFCUPlIiSp1nWoLDXxwQkqX9QjvLz6NeUbreod4mPT6Vqn1GZEZYrjiQCju5PR6JDAYHmLeodzI1ubQYB0x1mg4MzjtgjJpQQe63oKQXDsqS46Q9aqmRa2RTXdAWgsG2tAHQUjPRRqQBbz4PylFS8CW7SFMuXpLezcS0kWKO_gEtz3uCRUQ9VT7mv70Lso28IV_a9zYwM6lSm75RAAax61Z4w5JXQEcwhtxVb-BuMeDYRRo-RZf2Bb2EwxSqcqQ84Mg-LTI9/G1S7x8yOLy9AkrBIijrwTc1liTChbtFn/20)

name: Sarah McBride
subject: The White House just attacked me over my identity
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
Trump doesn’t care who’s actually doing a bad job — he cares about projecting an image of strength. I’m over it.
# [](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1269?t=1&akid=3240%2E21716%2E-Mr1Kl)
I recently pointed out to a reporter that Donald Trump only seems to fire women from his cabinet, regardless of how terrible a job men like Pete Hegseth are doing.
**Predictably, the White House jumped at the opportunity to distract their audience by coming after me over my identity.**
[****](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20260508-2.52y0QB&refcode=em20260508-3240&%3Bamount=5&t=2&akid=3240%2E21716%2E-Mr1Kl)
Schoolyard taunts aside, what I said remains as true as ever. Right now we are engaged in a reckless war of choice that Pete Hegseth was a primary driver of. Yet the only cabinet members Trump has actually fired are women.
Trump doesn’t care who’s actually doing a bad job — he cares about projecting an image of strength. I’m over it.
**I’m not here to play into the White House’s bigotry. No matter how much they attack me, I’m going to continue speaking out and standing up for what’s right for Delaware.**
[**Will you stand with me by pitching in $5 or any amount you can spare? Every dollar helps us fight back and stay focused on what’s really important.**](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20260508-2.52y0QB&refcode=em20260508-3240&amount=5&t=3&akid=3240%2E21716%2E-Mr1Kl)
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20260508-2.52y0QB&amount=5&refcode=em20260508-3240&t=22&akid=3240%2E21716%2E-Mr1Kl)**
Thank you,
Sarah McBride

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Victory: Court Rules AG Kris Mayes’ Office Broke Law
body:
Chinese Man Gets $59 Million in Covid Relief, Launders Half to China

[INSIDE JW]

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES FOR EMAILS BETWEEN FOIA OFFICER AND TOP HEALTH
OFFICIALS

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37789&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dS5iZS9wUldzQV9RLU0wMD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=c0121603175bde3e9566944ef199721b5757b72053d712a612d9c8f98c52cc60]

Key decisions were made behind closed doors at Dr. Anthony Fauci’s
federal health agency during the pandemic, and the American people
have a right to know what was hidden from them.

We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37762&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtaGhzLW1vcmVucy1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDE0OTEvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=360dc06a01a7b1c0ad65b05c67592620b4b20e1e1a23a4d762b8e24b48cf62fc]
for all email communications between National Institute of Allergy and
Infectious Diseases (NIAID) FOIA Officer Margaret Moore and top NIAID
officials, including former Director Dr. Anthony Fauci (_Judicial
Watch, Inc. v. U.S. Department of Health and Human Services_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37763&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtaGhzLW1vcmVucy1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDE0OTEvXV8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=7df9ad4395b584ec126d982681c7c72cc65e7d83f838245144afa5f7156fcd2f _(No.
1:26-cv-01491)).

Moore is the “FOIA Lady” who allegedly taught
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37764&F=T&&hash=5197ca5a8d4d62fd99780cb2d9a4da8a582207ff2f57327ee72b6413b4e7d886]
David Morens, pandemic-era NIAID senior advisor to Fauci, “how to
make emails disappear after I am foia’d” to cloak top NIAID
officials’ communications from ever seeing the light of day.

We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after
the National Institutes of Health (NIH) failed to respond to a June 4,
2024, FOIA request for all email communications between Moore (the
“FOIA Lady”) and any of the following:

> NIAID Director Anthony Fauci
>
> NIAID Senior Advisor David Morens
>
> NIH Bioethics Official Christine Grady
>
> NIAID Principal Deputy Director Hugh Auchincloss, M.D.
>
> NIAID Deputy Director H. Richard Lane
>
> EcoHealth Alliance President Peter Daszak

In June 2023 the House Select Subcommittee on the Coronavirus
Pandemic, announced
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37765&F=T&&hash=c981f878298a5b2e23d10d8763e03c55c21197a7441eea69f61875b1075c86ad]
its investigation of “a potential violation of federal record
keeping laws” by Morens at the NIAID. The subcommittee chair wrote:
“In one e-mail you write that you ‘always try to communicate over
gmail because my NIH email is FOIA’d constantly.’ You continue,
‘[d]on’t worry, just send to any of my addresses and I will delete
anything I don’t want to see in the _New York Times_.’”

As part of the expanding investigation into NIH records practices,
congressional investigators began examining the role of FOIA
processing officials in handling or advising on records requests
involving senior agency personnel.

Moore came to congressional attention
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37764&F=T&&hash=5197ca5a8d4d62fd99780cb2d9a4da8a582207ff2f57327ee72b6413b4e7d886]
in 2024 during the subcommittee’s investigation into NIH
communications practices and alleged efforts to avoid federal records
disclosure requirements. In September 2024, the subcommittee sent a
letter
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37766&F=T&&hash=60a8d6ce16898ef89bd0c71a9458d05ccd5159d6e98df86eb3fdaaca0515e0ee]
and subpoena to Moore regarding Morens’ communications. Her attorney
informed Congress
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37767&F=T&&hash=47aa50850bb4c5d35a15e376e7bcff2511a519797bded1ce39a518814325db11]
that she intended to invoke the Fifth Amendment.

In April 2026, the U.S. Department of Justice announced
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37768&F=T&&hash=071cbbf57ad7733fc91483bd9ffb7cf689018cb670593c0bcd814a7e70590e5a]
that Morens was indicted
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37769&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3VzYS12LW1vcmVucy1pbmRpY3RtZW50LTAwMTM4Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=a0cf8a2f645f2664c21a6d8a81a5e940051a9a42bf07fa5d7645c4ef2ee31604]
for allegedly helping to conceal and destroy federal records, evading
FOIA requests, and using personal email to hide Covid-related
communications during the pandemic. The indictment references a March
2021 Judicial Watch FOIA lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37770&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbmloLXd1aGFuLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=bf92256a1fc9d84c331ad299be5002f0db28ff7383013d2b3a7138693b5613b8] that uncovered
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37771&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdGFnLzAwNjk2Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=4addab8a358f8e18fa44aae482f674750d9a3f483ec5285067f81cbbbd22fe43] many NIH communications,
contracts and agreements with the Wuhan Institute of Virology in

Our FOIA lawsuits and investigations have uncovered much of what the
public knows about many Covid-19 controversies:

In April 2026, we sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37612&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcmVjb3Jkcy1vbi1jb3ZpZC0xOS1vcmlnaW5zLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=a9ba68c0400ec0f4ec0349b90038ebbbb318a088da2174d1f415b082ad6d1df8] the
Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI) for records and
communications of its employees with other government officials
regarding coronaviruses, the Wuhan Institute of Virology (WIV), and
related Covid-19 research.

In March 2026, we sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37267&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZ2Fpbi1vZi1mdW5jdGlvbi1yZWNvcmRzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=5e896981a2b71ec196bee94abb315b9caeebb4a19a610ef805dfc8b87f0e267b] the U.S.

The Biological Technologies Office was launched
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37257&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZGFycGEubWlsL25ld3MvMjAxNC9iaW9sb2dpY2FsLXRlY2hub2xvZ2llcy1vZmZpY2UtbGF1bmNoP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=110b2450f7c2c3b1403dc194a99ee4b751102a4065dea7abaf12e28c0cc9952f]

In June 2025, we sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=35471&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY292aWQtMTktd3VoYW4tZ2FtZXMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=63f22377bf65a95ebd19091b34bd401bd7c7338fba2bf6b473c9831dd6180604] the Defense
Department for all records regarding U.S. military personnel possibly
contracting Covid-19 in October 2019 during the World Military Games
in Wuhan, China. The lawsuit cited a December 2022 report
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=35428&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9kb3dubG9hZC5taWxpdGFyeW9uZXNvdXJjZS5taWwvMTIwMzgvTU9TL1JlcG9ydHMvMjAxOS1Xb3JsZE1pbGl0YXJ5R2FtZXMtUmVwb3J0RGVjMjAyMi5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=b770013c9b1805ae4d9ac9bc7e4f75c79ca6cfee7f005405a7ee712dce92fd17]
issued by the Pentagon titled “Report to the Committees on Armed

In May 2025, we received records
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=35153&F=T&&hash=21bea7b95ae9095b20ce9e67818c190bb4f09a87bf2d7f9d01d39737b18cab19]
from the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) that
revealed its plans to mandate Covid-19 vaccinations for 17 million
health care employees and that only one of 4,682 claims for injuries
and deaths due to Covid-19 “countermeasures” at the time was
compensated.

Records uncovered in 2024 from the Federal Bureau of Investigation
(FBI) through a FOIA request showed an April 2020 email exchange with

the Wuhan Institute of Virology in China as including

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=34195&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZnVuZGVkLWdhaW4tb2YtZnVuY3Rpb24td3VoYW4vXeKAnT91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=43f3e16f972ccf5abe4479629acde1d700c418fe95aaf9b89669fe8f44d6d249
which “would leave no signature of purposeful human manipulation.”

Records [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=29366&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY29yb25hdmlydXMtbXV0YW50cy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=57e925017bbbcefa1929b0782d0eb7f159c85cee56b2eb55d0c73e6449d5b54a] from the

Records

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=25283&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZWNvaGVhbHRoYWxsaWFuY2Uub3JnLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=a2f7843494dfe4c09b6808af41b0cba774cba886ff99c11d81147bc10b67372a] lack of compliance with reporting

Health and Human Services records included an “urgent for Dr. Fauci

Health and Human Services records included a grant application for

In its opinion
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81382&L=37737&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtYXotYWctbWF5ZXMtYXBwZWFscy1vcGluaW9uLTAwNTczMi0wMzIyLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=d8ceb1c5ff7b2a4371fb50513ffc36901d108704f043a005388332e62af5ad07],
the Arizona Court of Appeals agreed with us about the withholdings and
the failure of the lower court to consider our arguments:

> At a status conference in May 2025, Judicial Watch asked the trial
> court to require the attorney general’s office to provide
> additional information regarding the assertedly privileged
> documents. The court declined to do so. It apparently concluded that
> the office had provided a sufficient index identifying those
> documents.
>
> ***
>
> As noted, the attorney general’s office supplied Judicial Watch
> with an index of assertedly privileged documents that were otherwise
> responsive to Judicial Watch’s request. The index contained two
> entries. Those entries referred collectively to twenty-one emails
> and twenty-nine attachments that the office had withheld.
>
> ***
>
> Here, the index provided by the attorney general’s office supplies
> no context about the withheld emails that would allow a court or any
> other party to determine if a privilege applies. The index therefore
> falls short ... It follows that the office has not made a prima
> facie showing of privilege.... (existence of attorney-client
> relationship required to show privilege). We thus agree with
> Judicial Watch that the privilege log’s insufficiency prevented
> the trial court from adequately scrutinizing the office’s
> privilege assertions.
>
> ***
>
> Judicial Watch additionally argues that the trial court erred by not
> analyzing whether the attorney general’s office properly redacted
> the names of States United employees “principally responsible for
> the engagement” from the engagement letter. The unredacted
> portions of the letter do not provide any context as to these
> employees’ roles within States United. Even assuming that they
> were attorneys and the office had validly claimed privilege, an
> attorney’s identity generally falls outside the privilege’s
> protections.... And, to the extent the office might have some valid
> basis for the redaction, neither the index nor the context of the
> letter itself supplies it.
>
> ***
>
> We therefore conclude that the attorney general’s office has not
> met its burden of showing that it adequately searched its records
> ... The office was not entitled to disregard Judicial Watch’s
> unambiguous records request in favor of its own, narrower
> interpretation.
>
> ***
>
> For the foregoing reasons, we vacate the trial court’s judgment
> and remand this case for further proceedings consistent with this
> opinion.

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES FOR RECORDS ON NEW JERSEY ICE FACILITY BREACH

You’ll recall the day Newark Mayor Ras Baraka and three Democratic
members of Congress breached the Delaney Hall Immigration and Customs
Enforcement (ICE) detention facility in New Jersey.

We are seeking all audio and video from the scene of this incident,
which could have easily escalated. The continued anti-ICE rhetoric

clean_body: Chinese Man Gets $59 Million in Covid Relief, Launders Half to China [INSIDE JW] JUDICIAL WATCH SUES FOR EMAILS BETWEEN FOIA OFFICER AND TOP HEALTH OFFICIALS [] Key decisions were made behind closed doors at Dr. Anthony Fauci’s federal health agency during the pandemic, and the American people have a right to know what was hidden from them. We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit [] for all email communications between National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID) FOIA Officer Margaret Moore and top NIAID officials, including former Director Dr. Anthony Fauci (Judicial Watch, Inc. v. U.S. Department of Health and Human Services [ (No. 1:26-cv-01491)). Moore is the “FOIA Lady” who allegedly taught [] David Morens, pandemic-era NIAID senior advisor to Fauci, “how to make emails disappear after I am foia’d” to cloak top NIAID officials’ communications from ever seeing the light of day. We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after the National Institutes of Health (NIH) failed to respond to a June 4, 2024, FOIA request for all email communications between Moore (the “FOIA Lady”) and any of the following: NIAID Director Anthony Fauci NIAID Senior Advisor David Morens NIH Bioethics Official Christine Grady NIAID Principal Deputy Director Hugh Auchincloss, M.D. NIAID Deputy Director H. Richard Lane EcoHealth Alliance President Peter Daszak In June 2023 the House Select Subcommittee on the Coronavirus Pandemic, announced [] its investigation of “a potential violation of federal record keeping laws” by Morens at the NIAID. The subcommittee chair wrote: “In one e-mail you write that you ‘always try to communicate over gmail because my NIH email is FOIA’d constantly.’ You continue, ‘[d]on’t worry, just send to any of my addresses and I will delete anything I don’t want to see in the New York Times.’” As part of the expanding investigation into NIH records practices, congressional investigators began examining the role of FOIA processing officials in handling or advising on records requests involving senior agency personnel. Moore came to congressional attention [] in 2024 during the subcommittee’s investigation into NIH communications practices and alleged efforts to avoid federal records disclosure requirements. In September 2024, the subcommittee sent a letter [] and subpoena to Moore regarding Morens’ communications. Her attorney informed Congress [] that she intended to invoke the Fifth Amendment. In April 2026, the U.S. Department of Justice announced [] that Morens was indicted [] for allegedly helping to conceal and destroy federal records, evading FOIA requests, and using personal email to hide Covid-related communications during the pandemic. The indictment references a March 2021 Judicial Watch FOIA lawsuit [] that uncovered [] many NIH communications, contracts and agreements with the Wuhan Institute of Virology in China. Our FOIA lawsuits and investigations have uncovered much of what the public knows about many Covid-19 controversies: In April 2026, we sued [] the Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI) for records and communications of its employees with other government officials regarding coronaviruses, the Wuhan Institute of Virology (WIV), and related Covid-19 research. In March 2026, we sued [] the U.S. Department of Defense (War Department) for records on funding proposals submitted to the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA) Biological Technologies Office prior to the Covid-19 outbreak. The Biological Technologies Office was launched [] in 2014. In June 2025, we sued [] the Defense Department for all records regarding U.S. military personnel possibly contracting Covid-19 in October 2019 during the World Military Games in Wuhan, China. The lawsuit cited a December 2022 report [] issued by the Pentagon titled “Report to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and House of Representatives: 2019 World Military Games” which states that seven “service members who attended the games exhibited Covid-19-like signs and/or symptoms” during the time surrounding their attendance at the games. In May 2025, we received records [] from the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) that revealed its plans to mandate Covid-19 vaccinations for 17 million health care employees and that only one of 4,682 claims for injuries and deaths due to Covid-19 “countermeasures” at the time was compensated. Records uncovered in 2024 from the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) through a FOIA request showed an April 2020 email exchange with several officials in the bureau’s Newark Field Office referring to a National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID) grant to the Wuhan Institute of Virology in China as including “gain-of-function research [ which “would leave no signature of purposeful human manipulation.” Records from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) showed that a Pfizer study surveyed 23 people in 2021 to gauge reactions to its Covid vaccine booster before asking the FDA [] to approve it. Records [] from the Health and Human Services included the initial grant application and annual reports to the National Institutes of Health (NIH) from EcoHealth Alliance [], describing the aim of its work with the Wuhan Institute of Virology to create mutant viruses “to better predict the capacity of our CoVs [coronaviruses] to infect people.” Health and Human Services records included emails [] of then-Director of the National Institutes of Health Francis Collins showing a British physicians’ group recommended the use of Ivermectin to prevent and treat Covid-19. Records [] from Health and Human Services regarding data Moderna submitted to the Food and Drug Administration on its mRNA Covid-19 vaccine indicated a “statistically significant” number of rats were born with skeletal deformations after their mothers were injected with the vaccine. The documents also revealed Moderna elected not to conduct a number of standard pharmacological studies on the laboratory test animals. Food and Drug Administration records detailed pressure [] for Covid-19 vaccine booster approval and use. National Institutes of Health records revealed [] an FBI “inquiry” into the NIH’s controversial bat coronavirus grant tied to the Wuhan Institute of Virology. The records also show National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID) officials were concerned about “gain-of-function” research in China’s Wuhan Institute of Virology in 2016. The Fauci agency was also concerned about EcoHealth Alliance’s [] lack of compliance with reporting rules and use of gain-of-function research in the NIH-funded research involving bat coronaviruses in Wuhan, China. Health and Human Services records revealed that from 2014 to 2019, $826,277 [] was given to the Wuhan Institute of Virology for bat coronavirus research by the NIAID. National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases records showed that it gave nine China-related grants to EcoHealth Alliance [] to research coronavirus emergence in bats and was the National Institutes of Health’s top issuer of grants to the Wuhan lab itself. The records also included an email from the vice director of the Wuhan Lab asking an NIH official for help finding disinfectants for decontamination of airtight suits and indoor surfaces. Health and Human Services records included an “urgent for Dr. Fauci [ email chain, citing ties between the Wuhan lab and the taxpayer-funded EcoHealth Alliance []. The government emails also reported that the foundation of U.S. billionaire Bill Gates worked closely with the Chinese government to pave the way for Chinese-produced medications to be sold outside China and help “raise China’s voice of governance by placing representatives from China on important international councils as high level commitment from China.” Health and Human Services records included a grant application for research involving the coronavirus that appeared to describe “gain-of-function [ research involving RNA extractions from bats, experiments on viruses, attempts to develop a chimeric virus and efforts to genetically manipulate the full-length bat SARSr-CoV WIV1 strain molecular clone. Health and Human Services records showed the State Department and the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases knew immediately in January 2020 that China was withholding Covid-19 data [], which was hindering risk assessment and response by public health officials. University of Texas Medical Branch (UTMB) records [] showed the former director of the Galveston National Laboratory at the University of Texas Medical Branch (UTMB), Dr. James W. Le Duc [] warned Chinese researchers at the Wuhan Institute of Virology of potential investigations into the Covid issue by Congress. Health and Human Services records regarding biodistribution studies and related data for the Covid-19 vaccines showed a key component of the vaccines developed by Pfizer/BioNTech, lipid nanoparticles (LNPs), were found outside the injection site [], mainly the liver, adrenal glands, spleen and ovaries of test animals, eight to 48 hours after injection. Records from the Federal Select Agent Program (FSAP) revealed safety lapses [] and violations at U.S. biosafety laboratories that conduct research on dangerous agents and toxins. Health and Human Services records included emails [] between National Institutes of Health (NIH) then-Director Francis Collins [] and Fauci, the director of National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases, about hydroxychloroquine and Covid-19. Health and Human Services records showed that National Institutes of Health officials tailored confidentiality forms [] to China’s terms and that the World Health Organization (WHO) conducted an unreleased, “strictly confidential” Covid-19 epidemiological analysis in January 2020. Fauci emails [] included his approval of a press release supportive of China’s response to the 2019 novel coronavirus. VICTORY: COURT RULES AG KRIS MAYES’ OFFICE FAILED TO FOLLOW THE LAW We’re shedding light on left-wing election shenanigans in Arizona. The Arizona Court of Appeals overturned [] a lower court’s decision that allowed Arizona State Attorney General Kris Mayes to hide documents about anti-Trump legal actions with a controversial outside left-wing group. The decision cited failures by the Mayes’ office to both properly search for and justify the secrecy of records concerning its coordination with States United Democracy Center []. A spokesman for Mayes told a media outlet the office would not appeal [] the ruling. We filed the lawsuit [] in February 2025 in Maricopa County Superior Court after the attorney general’s office failed to comply with a public records request seeking records about its dealings with the States United Democracy Center and the Voter Protection Program [] (Judicial Watch Inc. v. Mayes [] (CV 2025-005732)). The request included records concerning discussions about investigating electors or challenging the 2020 election involving Donald Trump. The attorney general’s office produced only limited records [] in December 2024. It withheld additional documents, citing attorney-client and work-product privilege—but provided no explanations for the withholdings as required under Arizona law. In its opinion [], the Arizona Court of Appeals agreed with us about the withholdings and the failure of the lower court to consider our arguments: At a status conference in May 2025, Judicial Watch asked the trial court to require the attorney general’s office to provide additional information regarding the assertedly privileged documents. The court declined to do so. It apparently concluded that the office had provided a sufficient index identifying those documents. * As noted, the attorney general’s office supplied Judicial Watch with an index of assertedly privileged documents that were otherwise responsive to Judicial Watch’s request. The index contained two entries. Those entries referred collectively to twenty-one emails and twenty-nine attachments that the office had withheld. Here, the index provided by the attorney general’s office supplies no context about the withheld emails that would allow a court or any other party to determine if a privilege applies. The index therefore falls short ... It follows that the office has not made a prima facie showing of privilege.... (existence of attorney-client relationship required to show privilege). We thus agree with Judicial Watch that the privilege log’s insufficiency prevented the trial court from adequately scrutinizing the office’s privilege assertions. Judicial Watch additionally argues that the trial court erred by not analyzing whether the attorney general’s office properly redacted the names of States United employees “principally responsible for the engagement” from the engagement letter. The unredacted portions of the letter do not provide any context as to these employees’ roles within States United. Even assuming that they were attorneys and the office had validly claimed privilege, an attorney’s identity generally falls outside the privilege’s protections.... And, to the extent the office might have some valid basis for the redaction, neither the index nor the context of the letter itself supplies it. We therefore conclude that the attorney general’s office has not met its burden of showing that it adequately searched its records ... The office was not entitled to disregard Judicial Watch’s unambiguous records request in favor of its own, narrower interpretation. * For the foregoing reasons, we vacate the trial court’s judgment and remand this case for further proceedings consistent with this opinion. (A November 2024 report [] revealed correspondence between the Arizona attorney general’s office and the nonprofit States United Democracy Center in the lead-up to the state’s prosecution tied to Donald Trump’s 2020 presidential campaign.) We are excited that the court has slapped back the unlawful secrecy about anti-Trump lawfare abuse by Arizona’s AG Kris Mayes. We are pushing hard in court for full disclosure about the details of what looks to be a conspiracy by the AG’s office to punish Trump supporters for exercising their fundamental rights. In November 2026, we received records [] from the Michigan Department of Attorney General in a Michigan Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request that show the state’s coordination with the nonprofit States United Democracy Center, which pushed indictments [] of President Donald Trump’s supporters, lawyers, activists, and Republican Party officials who disputed the 2020 election. Indictments of Trump alternative electors by Michigan Democratic Attorney General Dana Nessel were thrown out [] by a judge earlier this year. The documents suggest a broad conspiracy by several states to prosecute Americans over 2020 election disputes. In April 2026, we received records [] from the State of Nevada Office of Attorney General in a Nevada Public Records Act lawsuit that show the state’s close coordination with States United Democracy Center. We continue to work to obtain records from Wisconsin []. JUDICIAL WATCH SUES FOR RECORDS ON NEW JERSEY ICE FACILITY BREACH You’ll recall the day Newark Mayor Ras Baraka and three Democratic members of Congress breached the Delaney Hall Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) detention facility in New Jersey. We are seeking all audio and video from the scene of this incident, which could have easily escalated. The continued anti-ICE rhetoric from the radical Left is putting lives in danger. We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit [] against the U.S. Department of Homeland Security seeking audio and video recordings, as well as inter-agency communications, related to a May 9, 2025, breach (Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of Homeland Security [ (No. 1:26-cv-01539)). We sued after Homeland Security failed to respond to a November 2025 FOIA request for: All audio recordings or video footage of the May 2025 incident at the Delaney Hall Detention Center in New Jersey during which public officials and other protesters attempted to enter the facility illegally. All records of communication between any official or employee of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement and any official or employee of any federal branch, department, or agency regarding the incident. Baraka was arrested at the scene for trespassing and released hours later. Representatives Rob Menendez Jr., Bonnie Watson Coleman, and LaMonica McIver—also present with a group of protesters—entered the facility grounds when security gates opened to admit an ICE bus. The Department of Homeland Security stated [ “These members of Congress storming into a detention facility goes beyond a bizarre political stunt and puts the safety of our law enforcement agents and detainees at risk. Members of Congress are not above the law and cannot illegally enter detention facilities. Had these members requested a tour, we would have facilitated a tour of the facility.” In June 2025, the U.S. Department of Justice announced [] that McIver “was charged in a three-count indictment for allegedly impeding and interfering with federal officers.” In February 2026, we filed a FOIA lawsuit [] for video footage and photographs of the attack on ICE’s Prairieland Detention Facility in Alvarado, TX, on July 4, 2025 (Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Homeland Security [ (No.1:26-cv-00331)). In December 2025, we sued [] the Department of Homeland Security for records on migrants who entered the United States from 2020–2025, using arrest warrants and removal orders as proof of identification. In November 2025, we sued Evanston, IL, Mayor Daniel Biss [] for records related to obstruction of federal immigration enforcement, as well as Arizona Gov. Katie Hobbs [] for records regarding her office reportedly ordering state police and the National Guard to withhold cooperation from federal immigration enforcement authorities. In December 2025 we pointed out [] that in just two states with “sanctuary” policies, nearly 9,000 criminal aliens were released from jails and prisons since January 20, defying Immigration and Customs Enforcement detainers to deport them. In October 2025, we reported [] on a Homeland Security Task Force (HSTF), established by Trump on the day of his inauguration to tackle a pandemic of transnational organized crime created by the Biden administration’s “disgraceful” open border policies, had made thousands of arrests and seized over 1,000 illegal firearms, 91 tons of drugs and $3 million in currency. CHINESE MAN GETS $59 MILLION IN COVID RELIEF, LAUNDERS HALF TO CHINA Are you at all surprised that when the government started giving out your tax dollars fraudsters saw an opportunity and acted? Our Corruption Chronicles blog reports []. Though it has been years since half a dozen laws were passed to dole out trillions of dollars in COVID-19 relief, fraud and corruption continue to plague the mammoth pandemic cash giveaway with a recent case involving a California man of Chinese descent who fraudulently collected tens of millions of dollars and laundered the proceeds to China. It is like a kick in the gut for American taxpayers, who continue to get fleeced all these years later by the pandemic measures—including the $2.1 trillion Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act [] (CARES Act) and $1.9 trillion American Rescue Plan [ were sold as necessary to provide economic relief to workers, families, small business, industry sectors and others affected by the 2020 public health crisis. The reality is that corruption is so rampant in all the pandemic relief programs that the Department of Justice (DOJ) created a special COVID-19 Fraud Enforcement Task Force (CFETF) a few years ago to crack down on scams and recover some of the stolen funds. Shortly after its creation, the task force exposed over $2 billion lost [] [ fraud with more than 3,500 defendants criminally charged. That was two years ago and the problem continues. In this latest case Bruce Jin of Los Angeles operated a sophisticated scheme that presented fake businesses as legitimate ones that sold personal protective equipment during the COVID-19 pandemic, allowing them to exploit emergency relief funds. Federal prosecutors say Jin stole $59 million in pandemic relief funds and transferred more than half of it to China using accounts at banks across the United States created with stolen identities. This month Jin was sentenced to 12 years in prison for conspiracy to commit wire fraud and conspiracy to launder monetary instruments in the amount of approximately $59 million, according to an announcement [] [ by the DOJ. “Unnamed members of the conspiracy, including some believed to be located in China, established thousands of accounts at banks across the United States using the personal identifying information (“PII”) of identity theft victims,” the agency writes, adding that from there, fraudulent claims were generated and paid to the accounts. How could a years-long scheme that siphoned tens of millions of taxpayer dollars using the nation’s banking system avoid law enforcement detection? It is a question many have asked since Uncle Sam started handing out pandemic cash. Judicial Watch has investigated and reported extensively on the fraud, including government employees taking advantage of the lax oversight. A few years ago, Internal Revenue Service (IRS) employees were charged [] [ stealing over a million dollars from pandemic relief programs to buy luxury goods, fancy cars, and travel. They easily withdrew the money by filing several false loan applications with two of the federal stimulus programs launched under the CARES Act. The disgraced federal employees used the illegally obtained pandemic relief funds to buy expensive designer clothes (Gucci), a fancy imported car (Mercedes-Benz), jewelry, trips to Las Vegas, manicures, and massages. The leftover cash was deposited into personal investment accounts. Each of the defendants submitted multiple fraudulent applications seeking at least tens of thousands of dollars in relief funds. Even incarcerated felons have defrauded COVID relief programs from jail. A few years ago, a gang member serving 19 years in a California state prison operated a scheme that collected $25 million in pandemic unemployment benefits [] and another jailed convict used stolen identities to claim more than $1 million [] in unemployment payments. In the first scheme the 31-year-old inmate submitted millions of dollars in fraudulent pandemic unemployment claims using stolen personal information of other incarcerated individuals as well as other civilians. Of the $25 million in fraudulent claims the government actually paid out $5.5 million, and the money was used to buy vehicles, jewelry, and other items. In the million-dollar scam, another California state inmate collected pandemic unemployment relief money using the personal information, including Social Security numbers, of fellow prisoners and other individuals to submit fraudulent claims from his jail cell. In the claims the inmate wrote that candidates had lost jobs from businesses or were unable to find employment due to the pandemic. It was that easy. BIDEN JUDGE RULES MUSLIM TERRORIST HAS RIGHT TO KEEP HIJAB IN PRISON Islamic law is creeping into our society more and more these days. It’s in federal prisons now, as our Corruption Chronicles_ blog reveals []. In a troubling development, a terrorist front group that has gained tremendous influence by suing and lobbying to embed Islam in American public institutions has succeeded in getting the nation’s federal prison system to submit to Islamic law. The case involves the Council on American-Islamic Relations (CAIR), a radical Muslim charity that was named as a co-conspirator in a federal terror-finance case [] [ the Hamas front group Holy Land Foundation. CAIR was founded in 1994 by three Middle Eastern extremists (Omar Ahmad, Nihad Awad and Rafeeq Jaber) who ran the American propaganda wing of Hamas, known then as the Islamic Association for Palestine. For years CAIR has used the U.S. legal system and widespread pressure campaigns to influence various areas of American public life, most recently by strong-arming Maryland’s largest taxpayer-funded school district [] [ give students the day off in observance of a Muslim religious holiday known as Eid al-Fitr. Now the extremist group that claims to advance Muslim civil rights is celebrating a huge legal victory that will impact a central component of the U.S. criminal justice system, the Federal Bureau of Prisons [] [). The agency with over 33,000 employees oversees the custody of some 154,000 federal inmates throughout the country. The FOB as well as the state prison system bans hats and other head covers for security and safety reasons. A few years ago, a Somali Muslim woman, Muna Jama, was required to remove her hijab—a head scarf used as a symbol of modesty in the Islamic dress code—during booking and official prison identification photos were taken. Jama was convicted and sentenced to 12 years in federal prison [ conspiracy to provide material support to the Somali terrorist group al-Shabaab, which has strong ties to al-Qaeda. Jama helped operate an online network that raised and disbursed money to al-Shabaab for jihadists plots, military operations and safehouses. CAIR sued the BOP on the terrorist’s behalf, asserting that the prison system’s policy is unconstitutional and violates her religious rights. Forcing the convicted extremist to remove her hijab, photographing her uncovered and requiring her to carry that uncovered photo on an ID she must present throughout the prison everyday is a violation of federal law, according to the complaint []. Jama’s faith requires her to always wear a hijab when she is in mixed-gender spaces outside of her immediate family, the court document states, adding that her religious beliefs are deeply rooted in Islamic texts and teachings. “Her hijab is a pillar of her religious practice and integral to her identity as a Muslim woman,” CAIR’s lawsuit reads. Appearing in public or being photographed without the and having that photo accessible to strangers is a serious breach of Jama’s faith and deeply humiliating, the group states. As a result, the convicted terrorist “has sustained damages, and has suffered and continues to suffer mental anguish, physical and emotional distress, humiliation, and embarrassment,” according to the complaint. A Biden-appointed federal judge in Minnesota, Jeffrey M. Bryan, agreed and recently issued an order [] affirming the rights of incarcerated Muslim women to wear a hijab despite established security protocols banning all head covers. The judge also directs the FOB to destroy all of Jama’s booking photographs without her hijab, writing that “the retention of Jama’s uncovered photo further substantially burdens Jama’s religious exercise because the photo’s mere existence means a non-familial male could potentially view the photo, thereby violating Jama’s religious beliefs.” The case is the latest of many in which CAIR has altered the way American law enforcement agencies do their job. Years ago, an Atlanta jail caved into CAIR’s demand that Muslim inmates be allowed to wear hijabs []. [] Under Obama CAIR got the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) to purge anti-terrorism training curricula of material coined “offensive” to Muslims, a scandal uncovered by Judicial Watch and documented in an in-depth report []. The terror front group also got several local police departments and the U.S. military to eliminate anti-terrorism training materials [] and instructors deemed anti-Muslim. At CAIR’s request, Obama’s Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, General Martin Dempsey, actually ordered the U.S. military to “scour its training material to ensure it doesn’t contain anti-Islamic content.” Until next week... # [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=41286%2E3422442%2ETWJ7f-)
Peter,
Ballots for this election were mailed out this week, which means we need to make sure as many voters as possible hear our message before they cast their vote.
With our June primary just weeks away, we need to pull ahead to secure a top-two spot and make it to November’s election.
**[Will you rush $5 today to get our message in front of more Californians before they vote?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260508_3_ak.rERaQl&refcode=em20260508-41286&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=41286%2E3422442%2ETWJ7f-)**

From town halls in every corner of the state, to our TV ads, to standing up to corporate greed and Trump’s agenda on the debate stage, it’s all led to this moment.
I’ve spent my whole career asking the tough questions and fighting for working families. **Now, our campaign needs to turn out voters so we can deliver _real_ solutions.**
**Every day counts before the primary, Peter.** That’s why I’m counting on Porter Supporters to step up today.
**[I’d be honored if you chipped in $5 or whatever makes sense to you to help us reach voters where they are before they cast their votes.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260508_3_ak.rERaQl&refcode=em20260508-41286&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=5&akid=41286%2E3422442%2ETWJ7f-)**

Thank you,
Katie

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20260508-41286&amounts=&t=25&refcode2=41286_3422442_TWJ7f-&akid=41286%2E3422442%2ETWJ7f-)**

name: ALERT! ALERT! ALERT! (protectvoting.org)
subject: Dem lawmakers want Trump GONE!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
Sign our letter to join them >>>
| **BREAKING NEWS! BREAKING NEWS! BREAKING NEWS!** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/109124?t=6&akid=24255%2E878179%2ELG4y1d) [**Every day, Trump marches us closer and closer towards a dictatorship. But Democrats are demanding for his REMOVAL from the White House once and for all! That’s why we started an urgent letter demanding for action – ADD YOUR NAME NOW! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/109124?t=7&akid=24255%2E878179%2ELG4y1d) | [**SIGN YOUR NAME: TRUMP MUST GO! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/109125?t=8&akid=24255%2E878179%2ELG4y1d)
---
Dear Members of Congress,

As of today, I urgently demand you to remove Donald Trump from office.

Because of his erratic and unconstitutional behavior, our democracy is on the verge of collapse. _Millions of Americans are paying the price for his authoritarian agenda._

_→ He’s called for networks to be pulled down and for journalists to be fired.
→ He’s defunded public broadcasting and made drastic cuts to Social Security.
→ He’s even calling on his MAGA cronies to control our elections and steal our right to vote._

These Democrats spend billions of our dollars, and what do we get?
* A "high-speed rail" with zero track laid.
* Cities occupied by drug addicts and the mentally ill.

body:
No images? [Click here](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-e-akdiuz-didthutlhu-n/) [](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akdiuz-didthutlhu-r/) Patriot,Republicans across Wisconsin are uniting next week at the 2026 WisGOP Convention, our opportunity to endorse strong candidates, sharpen our winning strategy, and stand together as one powerful team.Momentum is building across the state. Republican enthusiasm is surging, and it grows stronger every day.Even greater energy is on the horizon. Our field team is mobilized and ready to hit the ground running, new initiatives are launching, and we are preparing a full-scale, coordinated effort to deliver victories up and down the ballot in November.Meanwhile, Wisconsin Democrats are in disarray. Their gubernatorial primary is fractured, with the leading candidate, Francesca Hong, polling at just 14%. As they fight over the soul of their party, the socialist wing is clearly gaining the upper hand.Our message is clear, confident, and rooted in Wisconsin values: We will protect our way of life and our family farms, deliver meaningful tax relief, and make Wisconsin more affordable for working families.This is our moment. Let's come together in strength, unity, and purpose, and get ready to win big in November.-Team WisGOP **Take Action!****We need Letter to the Editor Signers!**
Become a Letter to the Editor (LTE) signer and help get the Republican message out to every community across the state. LTE volunteers are critical to continuing to advance our Republican values. Our goal is to have groups in every county who submit LTEs regularly. These groups will have resources provided from our team, including talking points, draft LTEs, and submission instructions. If you have any questions, please reach out to our Communications Director, Anika Rickard, at [arickard@wisgop.org](mailto:arickard@wisgop.org). [BECOME A LTE SIGNER!](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akdiuz-didthutlhu-y/) **Become a Poll Observer!**
Missed the poll worker deadline? Don't worry, there's still time to become a poll observer. Sign up below! [BECOME A POLL OBSERVER!](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akdiuz-didthutlhu-j/) **Help us bring victories to Republican candidates!**
Campaigns take money, and we need YOUR help to fund campaigns at every level. While Democrats rely on out-of-state billionaires, Republicans are dedicated to the grassroots and the State of Wisconsin. Help us show Democrats that Republicans are the party Wisconsin voters trust by donating TODAY! [DONATE TODAY!](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akdiuz-didthutlhu-t/)
# **Top Shareable Content**

subject: Virginia Redistricting, New UFO Files, and Shipping Guns by Mail
body:
Virginia's Supreme Court on Friday [struck down the state's new congressional redistricting plan](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGPnDAQhX-N6byyxxhD4SIN3aVNiQweYCKDkT3s6v59tBelSH31e_r0vvfrY_oIlOjcfoYDPa95OvFVEzJjmZ5UNjopFIxUudDCdG4nvu4JFHRSWamGKQZKn1PbRL8iApoGvXauN6BB22b3cQFQw7pYo_vWaWfCPNvBRpgHZ6zWDfk3TVk1aKU6ax5Gta6NziH20YBbRKswEufy-J3p5B3X9JmfWB5LPprkd-arCvNDwChg5B13SumdCRgLbncKTPkUMC75LiznwJywChht3w2d7uQ_TVnvq-CB8m-Rc61Y5X_y8giX_JI_KDKW481pio_Xi1Ki2jnRqu0I9DWg4W89-vTwJwAA__-IKovB), delivering a major setback to Democrat in a nationwide gerrymandering battle against Republicans for an edge in this year's midterm elections.

## Court Overturns Democrats' Virginia Map
Virginia's Supreme Court on Friday [struck down the state's new congressional redistricting plan](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGPnDAQhX-N6byyxxhD4SIN3aVNiQweYCKDkT3s6v59tBelSH31e_r0vvfrY_oIlOjcfoYDPa95OvFVEzJjmZ5UNjopFIxUudDCdG4nvu4JFHRSWamGKQZKn1PbRL8iApoGvXauN6BB22b3cQFQw7pYo_vWaWfCPNvBRpgHZ6zWDfk3TVk1aKU6ax5Gta6NziH20YBbRKswEufy-J3p5B3X9JmfWB5LPprkd-arCvNDwChg5B13SumdCRgLbncKTPkUMC75LiznwJywChht3w2d7uQ_TVnvq-CB8m-Rc61Y5X_y8giX_JI_KDKW481pio_Xi1Ki2jnRqu0I9DWg4W89-vTwJwAA__-IKovB), delivering a major setback to Democrats in a nationwide gerrymandering battle against Republicans for an edge in this year's midterm elections. The court ruled that the legislature did not follow proper procedures when it placed a constitutional amendment on the ballot authorizing the mid-decade redistricting. The decision overturns a map that would've given Democrats a 10-1 tilt in Virginia, leaving the existing 6-5 map in place.

An analyst at the nonpartisan Cook Political Report predicted that nationally, redrawn maps [will help the GOP](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuhDAQRL_GdD6ZNWCucJGG7tKmRJa9cBsZG63Nnfj7iEj5gjTTjPQ0b74e88NRpLR-ug1tXfKc8F0i1oo8v4hXSuQYA5XK5CulNeH7mEHBIFUv1X0OjuI5d02wCyKgbtC2xowaWmj75mmhxwXvgF4Z1frBjcPoBhg6bULQizYN2YumenVvlRp6fdOqM10wBnEMGowXncJANfPtO1OqT1zimV_IN5-3JtpnrXsR-kPAJGBK555LvSoB0wUWMKn-ilHAdBR5-QmY_txkOXbGDaXPB1d5cfmIWGTALXt2lbxckfncXArIAqaGbdjfFCOVwYhOrZuj-Lul_uvAl4WfAAAA___9woUp) in states including Alabama, Florida, Louisiana, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Texas, and projects that Republicans "will pick up somewhere in the six to seven-seat range from redistricting."
## Cyberattack Hits 9,000 Schools
A cyberattack [knocked a major international learning platform](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL8GMkZtG9tD4OCSyebSC5GxG-hbjxnZDSP-fsX-woalkkr13r_n-PSUKC9__Qsdz9uY8VMTMmMZDyoLZfIFI1UuFJjykvGzjxKk6UB3MIzRUzrHvo1uRpSoWnTC2ruSQgrdrk5DULqfPFol_KxnRIFyAgwGJFoLLblrDTQMAsBodVPQ2z5ai3iPStrQ9ICReCu3_xtlXnFO53ZguYXt1Sa3Mr9ro_408tHIh39fAFd1hcIUEjbyEc4Ji2f24aurYd22VLvg8-FrR7ly2QPvBbu6Uj7XPTOW2nmI1orB3AdpFJpJzwNEQKOUGsSkp7a4-P5QSlSNbXpYXp7Szyn-lcnDye8AAAD___qfiJk) offline on Thursday, forcing final exam postponements and endangering the data security of 275 million students, teachers, and staff across nearly 9,000 schools worldwide. A hacking group calling itself ShinyHunters struck Canvas, a widely used learning management platform, during finals week for many schools. Canvas parent company Instructure announced the [platform has now been restored](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1uxCAUhE-DOyz-vHgLijTuNm1KC8PDfgmGFbC29vaRc4V0I430ab75eswPixHT-ml3MC3kOcFZI7QGZT6wrJjQFvBYW0HXMK0JztcsmLhRNlB2n73F-J5V500AECA7MFzrUQou-NBthttBOLb4oAVIUF7LcRyWwHRwii_cd2guGhvYnTN2G2QvmdLKaw0weim0I4qBx5ZL_50xtQ1CfOcDSu_y3kWztfasRH4QMRExnefZu6VeFldPxHRFIib3XqDY1qz7oXV7XYKVOpsOW2kEWxKmlT6jbSGXnYZcaHVbzrFS60qulb4uSleMf54YI9abJoqtu8X4N6T9673DiN8AAAD__9Y8h1M) for most users. The University of Illinois [postponed all final exams](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGSrCAQRb8GMyxsVMaA4CVm89INrR5otXcRLGF05--3Zn9h81u3zjkf9-mOHDgu_3EjW-Y0RbpyoFLomE4-Fo6MB3nO5WBXOC6RrucECnqpOqmGySOH19RW3s5EQLoi2xhz09BA01Wr1Tczt15r5x99_-hBd-ibGTvUCtquURXb95vq1NAo1Xe61qo1rTeG6OY1GCdaRZ5LOurPxLGsNIdXOumoXdqqYNdS9iz0PwGjgPG6rjo-nFvZ4ZLeEwHj20nAGJLDIGB8yjRLlnvKZU-Rspw5YpD0jVuW_kmyJOkwnpjliu5LwKgHrfWgBIzVYf1-cQiceyNatWzI4Zek_CnfaeEnAAD__yGIg6o) and assignments for several days, while the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, moved May 8 exams to May 9. The University of Minnesota also alerted students to the breach, warning that hackers threatened to release personal information of millions of users. Instructure said it [found no evidence](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1uhDAUhE8DHcg_GEPhIg3dpk2JjP2Alxh7Zb8F7e0jcoV0I400mu_7eswPiwHj9mkPMLSmOcJVAhBBnk_MG0a0GTwWyugI4xbhes2Cib5hqmHj7C2G99zV3qwAAmQNhms9SMEFV_VuFia11qAdX1a9jIPio_C850J2fhkZq9Hca0yxkTPWK9lK1unOaw0weCm0qzoGHinl9jthpB3W8E4n5Nalow5mJ3qWSn5UYqrEdF1X-7Z7SndbienGqcRkM6ELcEdn42lLs1v306wpOygNQYxQCkDzinhCLkgIpeFSKd7zYWx3OkKdjX9eGAKWXlcd2w6L4e8D_UvcacRvAAAA__9Vi4K0) that passwords, dates of birth, government IDs, or financial information were compromised, and that its investigation remains ongoing.
## Government Releases New UFO Reports
The Trump administration on Friday began [releasing thousands of previously classified files](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGO8yAUhE-DO1v4AcYpKP7GXf52SwvDw3krAhEQW7n9ynuF7Uaa0Yzm-7qvd0uR0v7fPtG0kNeEZ43YGpb1oLJTIlvQU22FXKO0JzzfK3CYeq56flu9pfhZZedNQAQUHZpR61nACKPqHkbyaQwhzPPmHYybU2HSINzNqVlyVHNH5mrjit9GziclBsGlll5rxNkL0I5Jjp5aLsN3ptQeGOInH1gGl59dNI_WXpWJfwwWBst5noPb6vXi8hksl2SwvDA1u-fUb7hTqn3BiLZi_w65DxTxynTF-NdJMVKdNJN8f1qKvzPtT2wOAz8BAAD__92GeXA) on UFOs, now known as unidentified anomalous phenomena, or UAPs, as part of an ongoing declassification project. Among the newly released documents is a transcipt from the 1965 Gemini 7 mission, in which astronaut Frank Borman reported an unidentified object to mission control, saying ["We have a bogey at 10 o'clock high."](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOhCAURb8GOyaAKFpQbGM3225pWHno2yAYeOPEv9-4v7DNbW5ycu_5es5PhxHT-ul2sBTynOBdIxBBmU8sKyZ0BTxWKrgQpjXB-zUroXouOi7G2TuM16wbbwOAgrYBK40ZWiWV7JrNhm85BmnCuHgfIBg9tNJpPyzaCR2MbNDeNNGJUQrRd-2jFdpobwzA4FtlFqYFeKRcHj8ZE20Q4pVPKI8l7020G9FRWfvB1MTUlK4jV7orpqYbzNQkujsGpqZX5fc_pqYVdkzIT0TuKpWc3Isqr0cmAs9P2HCJwDHxergF-J4LcNpc4r3gF7hSuVszU1NTrD_eGCPW3jAt1t1h_FtG_9J5WvUbAAD__8wfics) The files also include a debriefing from the 1969 Apollo 11 moon mission. Buzz Aldrin described [seeing flashes of light](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2O6yAURldjd1j8GeyC4jXp8toprRtzbd8JgQhwUHY_ymxh-k-fzjlf1-UKFCju_-GBrm5pidhKwFoxLy_KO0WCjJ5KzbRWinvEdi6SS8P4yPi8eKDwXnTv3YYoUfXohLWTkkKKsT-cmozgsJlRiU1rpWc1qwluxsINVo6-J_d54yOfBedmVIPi2mpvLeLklbRrpzl6qikP34liPXAL7_TCPKzp0Qd31PosnfrXyUsnL6214aPQEO-fQScvECOwJ5yBIrBwRmAVoWBh55YYBMLIPJU1pHJmZDV5eLN2QGUN2T2mxoSYpTWj6rPzz0YhUDG203x_AIVfivqndC8nfwIAAP__CaqENQ) inside the cabin and a bright light source he tentatively attributed to a possible laser. The White House said additional declassified UFO records, videos, and government files are [still on the way](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1uhDAQxfHTQGdkxoChcJFmu02bEjl4gIn8gezZtfb20eYK6Z9-T_p_3de7JU_x-LQBDe9pjViLR2bM65PyQZFsRkeFM21M8YhYHytImIQchVxWZ8m_1qF1ZkcEVC2aXutZQQ_92J5GwTQ7mHcc-sn2OMtZg7YWp0Xp70VvLZm3Jke59FJOo-qUHPTgtEacnQK9NYNER5xy95Mo8om7f6Un5m5LofXmZL5Koz4auDVwq7V2gTyxza_3oIEb50e4RLowFvHYk9jJYxEUxUmFU6ZNHG8uBowsMnq0Bdts3FXJeyqTbgZ5BEv-75D_Velp4DcAAP__83J-Jg).

On this day in 1914, President Woodrow Wilson proclaimed to celebrate America's mothers, stating it was a chance to "publicly express our love and reverence for the mothers of our country." Many thanks to the hundreds of readers who offered us their favorite way to make eggs, ranging from custom family recipes like "choppy eggs" to standards like scrambled, over easy, or "baked into a cake." And now it turns out that eating eggs regularly could lower the risk of getting Alzheimer's. You can in our Et Cetera section below. In other news: A deadly virus just killed three people aboard a cruise ship, and passengers have now scattered to more than a dozen countries, including here in the United States. It's not COVID. It's not the flu. It's Hantavirus, and the way it spreads might already be hiding in your home. On today's episode of The Flyover Podcast , host Amy Hess reveals the five things every American needs to know before this gets any closer. ! Today's sponsor, , is sharing Dr. Steven Gundry's gut-health snack recommendation that may help support healthier digestion, reduce occasional bloating, and help you feel slimmer naturally. | Court Overturns Democrats' Virginia Map Virginia's Supreme Court on Friday , delivering a major setback to Democrats in a nationwide gerrymandering battle against Republicans for an edge in this year's midterm elections. The court ruled that the legislature did not follow proper procedures when it placed a constitutional amendment on the ballot authorizing the mid-decade redistricting. The decision overturns a map that would've given Democrats a 10-1 tilt in Virginia, leaving the existing 6-5 map in place. An analyst at the nonpartisan Cook Political Report predicted that nationally, redrawn maps in states including Alabama, Florida, Louisiana, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Texas, and projects that Republicans "will pick up somewhere in the six to seven-seat range from redistricting." Cyberattack Hits 9,000 Schools A cyberattack offline on Thursday, forcing final exam postponements and endangering the data security of 275 million students, teachers, and staff across nearly 9,000 schools worldwide. A hacking group calling itself ShinyHunters struck Canvas, a widely used learning management platform, during finals week for many schools. Canvas parent company Instructure announced the for most users. The University of Illinois and assignments for several days, while the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, moved May 8 exams to May 9. The University of Minnesota also alerted students to the breach, warning that hackers threatened to release personal information of millions of users. Instructure said it that passwords, dates of birth, government IDs, or financial information were compromised, and that its investigation remains ongoing. Government Releases New UFO Reports The Trump administration on Friday began on UFOs, now known as unidentified anomalous phenomena, or UAPs, as part of an ongoing declassification project. Among the newly released documents is a transcipt from the 1965 Gemini 7 mission, in which astronaut Frank Borman reported an unidentified object to mission control, saying The files also include a debriefing from the 1969 Apollo 11 moon mission. Buzz Aldrin described inside the cabin and a bright light source he tentatively attributed to a possible laser.
